chunk_summary: The POSHAN Tracker is a real-time monitoring tool for Anganwadi services, requiring clinicians to audit data quality by verifying anthropometric methods, service delivery, and dashboard plausibility to ensure accurate pediatric nutrition monitoring.
learning_objectives: Understand the functionality and purpose of the POSHAN Tracker in monitoring Anganwadi services.
Learn to identify and address common data quality issues in anthropometric measurements and service delivery records.
Develop skills to audit POSHAN Tracker outputs using a clinician-auditor checklist.
Apply methods to reconcile service receipt data with caregiver-reported history and clinical caseloads.
key_facts: POSHAN Tracker supports near real-time monitoring of Anganwadi services, including beneficiary tracking and growth indicators.
Data entry is performed via a mobile application with offline entry and later synchronization capabilities.
Common errors include incorrect length/height measurements, uncalibrated equipment, and inaccurate age entries, which can distort wasting severity classifications.
Clinicians should verify measurement methods (e.g., calibrated scales, correct MUAC technique) and repeat implausible extremes.
Dashboards showing improvement should be cross-verified with sentinel re-measurement samples and clinical caseloads.
Identity seeding is used to maintain data continuity for migrating families.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: ‘Full coverage’ dashboards can coexist with missed severe wasting when length/height technique is poor, equipment is not calibrated, age is entered incorrectly, or services are recorded but not actually delivered or consumed.
document_key_topics: POSHAN Tracker functionality and data entry process
Common errors in anthropometric measurements and service delivery data
Clinician-auditor checklist for data quality verification
Using POSHAN Tracker outputs for actionable insights in pediatric nutrition
